title: Evangelion 20 Cam Remix
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0 Cam Remix Post by BeautifulSeizure » Thu Dec 10, 2009 8:59 pm I was tired... Tired of waiting. I couldn't hold it anymore... I finally gave in to the temptation. Downloaded the infamous cam-rip. Went through the first 10 minutes or so. Feelings of anger arose. I had already decided I wanted to spoil the whole experience with sub-quality material. Unfortunately I was not ready to sink that low. The Evangelion 2.0 Cam Remix project was born. Preview video is in spoiler: SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 10, 2009 10:33 pm The last cam rip I had the displeasure of watching was one for Jurassic park back in 1993 or thereabouts. While I'm sure technology has improved since then, I doubt you will be able to make much headway with the material you've got. Personally, I can wait for the DVD release. In fact, I'll probably end up waiting for the 2.45 Blu-Ray release or whichever ends up being the definitive one. Anyway, while your project is perhaps interesting as an academic exercise in digital signals processing, this site needs must be paranoid about attracting the attention of legal copyright legions of doom, especially considering the thickness of the ice we're already tap dancing on. So unfortunately, I'm afraid I've had to lock the thread. Sorry. There may be various other venues to promote this project, but I'm afraid here isn't one of them. Incidentally, if you are indeed in possession of "mad skills" in the video cleanup department, there's a long standing need to have the Reiquarium images from episode #23' decoded.

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 10, 2009 10:33 pm The last cam rip I had the displeasure of watching was one for Jurassic park back in 1993 or thereabouts. While I'm sure technology has improved since then, I doubt you will be able to make much headway with the material you've got. Personally, I can wait for the DVD release. In fact, I'll probably end up waiting for the 2.45 Blu-Ray release or whichever ends up being the definitive one. Anyway, while your project is perhaps interesting as an academic exercise in digital signals processing, this site needs must be paranoid about attracting the attention of legal copyright legions of doom, especially considering the thickness of the ice we're already tap dancing on. So unfortunately, I'm afraid I've had to lock the thread. Sorry. There may be various other venues to promote this project, but I'm afraid here isn't one of them.
